Title: A cellulose synthase-like protein governs the biosynthesis of Solanum alkaloids

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Tomato leaves and green fruit; potato tubers
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies cholesterol directly to the GAME15-centered SGA metabolon, linking cholesterol biosynthesis with SGA production
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential supporting gene for metabolic channeling and flux optimization in engineered SGA production
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 7-DR2 physically associates with GAME15. 2. It likely provides the cholesterol precursor directly into the SGA metabolon.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as cholesterol-pathway component of the metabolon rather than the primary experimental focal gene

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Tomato leaves and green fruit; potato tubers
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with the GAME15-containing complex that connects cholesterol formation to SGA biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary target for pathway flux coupling in metabolic engineering
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C5-SD2 interacts/proximally associates with GAME15. 2. It likely participates in precursor supply within the cholesterol-SGA biosynthetic assembly.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supportive metabolon component, not primary causal gene of the study

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step; essential missing link enabling SGA pathway reconstitution and metabolic flux organization
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Tomato; Potato; Nicotiana benthamiana; Yeast; Insect cells
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Solanum lycopersicum; Solanum tuberosum; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Spodoptera frugiperda
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Tomato leaves and green fruit, especially skin; potato tuber outer layers
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream committed entry step linking cholesterol metabolism to SGA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucuronic acid from UDP-GlcA to cholesterol to form cholesterol glucuronide
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor and channels flux toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes SGA biosynthesis; silencing/knockout causes cholesterol accumulation, CHR-GlcA depletion, and strong reduction/loss of SGAs
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: cellulose synthase-like M (CslM) protein with multiple transmembrane domains
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Coexpressed with other GAME genes; peaks in green tissues rich in α-tomatine
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in green tomato tissues; associated with SGA-rich tissues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi silencing; CRISPR knockout; transient expression in N. benthamiana; yeast reconstitution; insect-cell expression; in vitro microsomal assay; isotope labeling with cholesterol-d7/d6; LC-MS; MALDI-MSI; FLIM-FRET; TAP-MS; split-LUC
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient agroinfiltration; cholesterol or cholesterol-d7/d6 feeding; insect herbivory assay
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense; contributes to pest resistance and is antinutritional to humans
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for reducing antinutritional SGAs in crops or reconstituting SGA/steroidal alkaloid production in heterologous hosts; requires caution because SGA depletion increases pest susceptibility
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced SGAs increase insect susceptibility; cholesterol accumulation and adverse developmental/reproductive effects reported upon silencing
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Tomato transcriptome coexpression analysis; TAP-MS proteomics
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAME15 is the missing link in Solanum SGA biosynthesis. 2. It glucuronidates cholesterol to CHR-GlcA, enabling downstream hydroxylation by GAME6/GAME8. 3. Loss of GAME15 depletes SGAs and causes cholesterol buildup. 4. GAME15 also acts as a scaffold for a cholesterol-SGA biosynthetic complex and enables pathway engineering in heterologous hosts.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shows strong cholesterol specificity in planta but broader sterol acceptance in yeast; cannot be functionally substituted by SOAP5 or quinoa CqCSL for SGA engineering

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; essential early pathway enzyme dependent on GAME15-mediated substrate entry
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Nicotiana benthamiana; Solanum pennellii; Solanum tuberosum
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Tomato green tissues/fruit skin; potato tubers
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates CHR-GlcA at C22 to form 22S-hydroxycholesterol glucuronide
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for SGA formation; silencing reduces α-tomatine/dehydrotomatine and causes CHR-GlcA accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Coexpressed with GAME15 and other GAME genes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression in N. benthamiana; acid hydrolysis with authentic standards; VIGS in S. pennellii; potato RNAi; LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful in pathway reconstruction with GAME15 for SGA engineering
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAME6 converts CHR-GlcA to 22S-hydroxycholesterol glucuronide. 2. It requires GAME15 presence for observable product formation in heterologous assays. 3. Silencing causes precursor accumulation and lowers SGA levels.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Potato ortholog PGA2 behaves similarly in insect cells, yeast, and plants

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; essential early pathway enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Nicotiana benthamiana; Solanum pennellii; Solanum tuberosum
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Tomato green tissues/fruit skin; potato tubers
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates sterol intermediate at C26 during early SGA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for SGA biosynthesis; silencing/knockout reduces SGAs and causes accumulation of upstream hydroxylated glucuronide intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient coexpression in N. benthamiana; hydrolysis/LC-MS; VIGS in S. pennellii; CRISPR knockout hairy roots in potato
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important for reconstituting early SGA pathway with GAME15
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAME8 catalyzes C26 hydroxylation of glucuronidated cholesterol intermediates. 2. It functions together with GAME15 and GAME6 to produce dihydroxylated intermediates. 3. Loss of function causes accumulation of 22-hydroxycholesterol-GlcA and reduced SGAs.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Potato PGA1/StGAME8 knockout hairy roots accumulated 22-hydroxycholesterol-GlcA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates pathway intermediate at C16 in SGA aglycone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing reduces α-tomatine/dehydrotomatine and causes accumulation of dihydroxycholesterol hexoses rather than dihydroxycholesterol-GlcA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; VIGS in S. pennellii; LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for completing SGA aglycone engineering
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAME11 catalyzes a later hydroxylation step in SGA biosynthesis. 2. Silencing lowers SGAs. 3. Data suggest GlcA removal may occur before or around the GAME11 step, although GAME11 can accept glucuronidated substrate in heterologous assays.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Interaction with GAME15 not consistently supported across all assays

Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GAME15 (split-LUC only, not confirmed by FLIM-FRET/TAP-MS)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes the C26 hydroxyl group of a later sterol intermediate in the SGA pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to tomatidenol/tomatidine formation in heterologous reconstitution when coexpressed with other GAME genes and GAME15
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required component for heterologous SGA aglycone production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAME4 functions in later aglycone-forming steps of SGA biosynthesis. 2. It is required in the multigene reconstitution producing tomatidenol/tomatidine with GAME15 present.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GAME4 does not show robust direct interaction with GAME15 in all assays

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: γ-aminobutyric acid transaminase involved in nitrogen incorporation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces nitrogen into the C26-oxidized sterol intermediate via GABA transamination in SGA aglycone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for tomatidine formation in heterologous reconstruction with other GAME genes and GAME15
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required module for engineered tomatidine production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAME12 acts in a late amination step of SGA biosynthesis. 2. It is necessary for tomatidine production in multigene pathway reconstitution.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper notes GAME4 and GAME12 do not require GAME15 in the monocot-enzyme bypass context
